text:
One sunny afternoon, a young man named Sam found himself at his favorite little boutique window-store humming a sweet tune while admiring the birds flying south for winter. He spotted a tiny, brilliant yellow bird perched on the edge of a branch.

Curious, Sam dashed inside just as the bird lifted off, fluttering gracefully towards the window. It landed on a branch outside, singing clearly.

"The robin!" he exclaimed.

A fellow shopper rushed out. "That's a mourning dove."

"No, no. It sings!" Sam insisted. He approached cautiously. "Hello?"

The bird bobbed its head. "Hellooo?"

Maybe he had a new friend.

Sam wondered how long it lived there. "You're really there?" he asked.

"Forever," the robin sang softly.

How fascinating.

The store owner greeted them. "That's just a regular birdie. It doesn't sing."

"A regular robin?" Sam asked?

"Uh yeah."

"That bird there... sings?" Sam said
